title: Information Retrieval
description: Study of information retrieval systems covering indexing, retrieval models, evaluation, and modern search engine architecture. Topics include inverted indexes, TF-IDF, BM25, vector space model, query processing, relevance feedback, web crawling, link analysis (PageRank), and introduction to learning-to-rank. CS301 is required for algorithm design and analysis skills essential for implementing efficient retrieval systems and understanding complexity of ranking algorithms.
topics: Evaluation metrics: precision, recall, MAP, NDCG
outcomes: Build inverted indexes for text collections
Implement TF-IDF and BM25 retrieval models
Evaluate retrieval systems using standard metrics
Implement web crawling and link analysis algorithms
Design and evaluate search engine components
